yes

**Reasoning:**
Mark Stone is a professional ice hockey player (currently with the Vegas Golden Knights). In ice hockey, players who commit infractions are sent to the "penalty box" to serve a penalty. The Stanley Cup is the championship trophy awarded to the winner of the National Hockey League (NHL) playoffs, which includes the Stanley Cup Finals. Therefore, it is entirely plausible for a player to spend time in the penalty box during a game that is part of the Stanley Cup playoffs.

*(Note: While the phrasing "in the Stanley Cup" is slightly imprecise—players are in the penalty box *during* a Stanley Cup playoff game, not inside the trophy itself—the core action of a hockey player being penalized in the context of the Stanley Cup playoffs is factually possible and plausible.)*<|im_end|>
